page: Microsoft Azure D4as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DASv5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, operating at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The architecture is x86_64, supported by 32 MB of L3 cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 32 KB of L1d cache. The system is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for remote disk attachments.

Benchmark results indicate a notable contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% of stress-ng benchmarks, and single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show average performance. However, multi-core execution is relatively weak, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores falling into the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth is mixed, showing average throughput for large block reads but weak performance for small block reads and large block writes. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput also fall into the lower performance tier.

Given its dedicated hardware allocation and balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the D4as_v5 provides a predictable entry-point for general-purpose workloads without the variable performance of burstable instances. It is best suited for lightweight applications, single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, and development environments that require dedicated CPU resources but do not demand high multi-threaded throughput or local high-speed storage.
description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this Premium Storage capable instance relies on remote disks. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25%, while multi-core performance, memory write bandwidth, and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. This resource profile makes the instance a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation but do not require heavy multi-threaded scaling. It is best suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded data processing, and development environments.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v5 is a general-purpose VM featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM. It offers strong single-core performance but exhibits weaker multi-core and database throughput.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4as_v5 is a general-purpose VM with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 16 GB RAM, offering strong single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A dedicated general-purpose virtual machine featuring strong single-core performance and a balanced memory-to-CPU ratio for predictable workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-vCPU AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory with a balanced 1:4 resource ratio
Delivers strong single-core CPU performance but weaker multi-core scaling
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or bundled GPUs
Suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded tasks, and development
